metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `merge_files: list[str]` (or `file_map: dict`) parameter to `assemble_merge_change_context` sourced from `get_merge_files`, so the `## Changed files` orientation section and the 'remaining files listed undiffed' truncation path both have the full Gerrit file list as input — not just paths parseable from the auto-merge diff text.
findings.finding: `assemble_merge_change_context(auto_merge_diff, mergelist)` is specified with only two parameters, but the merge-path file list (from `get_merge_files`) is not declared as an input parameter. The existing `compose_diff_context(changed_files, diff_text)` seam — which `assemble_merge_change_context` must call or replicate to produce the `## Changed files` orientation block — requires a `changed_files: list[str]` argument. Without it, the merge context either omits the file-list section (breaking the format contract) or silently re-parses files from the auto-merge diff text (missing files that appear in `get_merge_files` but have no diff lines in the auto-merge output, e.g. clean merges).
findings.location: S2 — 'Merge-context contract' paragraph; src/rebar/llm/code_review/assemble.py:77–93

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to S3's done-definition a specific verification that `checkout-gerrit-change-action` correctly checks out the full merge tree (both parents resolvable) for a merge-commit refspec — either by citing the action's documented merge-commit support or by capturing a run log that shows `git log --oneline -3` resolving both parent SHAs after checkout.
findings.finding: The S3 done-definition asserts that 'a merge change into main gets CI on the actual merge tree (checkout-gerrit-change-action fetching the merge refspec).' This relies on `checkout-gerrit-change-action` (a 1fa8 deliverable) being able to check out a merge-commit refspec (refs/changes/NN/CHANGE/REV where the patchset is a merge commit). This is an unverified external capability: a merge commit's `refs/changes/` ref fetches the merge commit itself, but the merged tree — the combination of feature branch + main — requires a checkout of FETCH_HEAD after fetching that ref. If `checkout-gerrit-change-action` simply does a `git checkout` without also establishing the parent refs, the checkout may succeed but the tree might be incomplete (parents missing for tooling that inspects history). The plan neither cites a doc for this action's merge-commit behavior nor mandates a fixture test of it.
findings.location: S3 — 'a merge change into main gets CI on the actual merge tree (checkout-gerrit-change-action fetching the merge refspec)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the g2p branch-filtering question before S3 begins and encode the answer as a concrete decision in the plan: either state 'g2p dispatches all branches by default — no ini change needed' (with a cited doc/source line) OR specify the exact `gerrit_to_platform.ini` `feature/*` stanza (section name, keys, values) that must be added, so an executor has a complete design regardless of which branch the investigation takes.
findings.finding: S3's done-definition defers a real design decision to the executor via a 'determine whether' placeholder: the plan says 'determine from g2p source/docs whether gerrit_to_platform.ini filters dispatch by branch' and offers two possible outcomes, one of which (filtering IS present and config IS needed) requires designing a `feature/*` stanza that is not specified anywhere in the plan. An executor who discovers filtering is active has no template, required keys, or acceptance criteria for the config change to write.
findings.location: S3 — CI coverage on feature branches + merge changes, done-definition first sentence
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'g2p dispatch decision' subsection under S3: 'Investigation (pre-story): run `grep -n branch gerrit_to_platform.ini` and inspect g2p source `dispatch()`. Expected outcome A (branch-agnostic): no ini change; record the confirming source line. Expected outcome B (filter present): add `[branch "feature/*"]` stanza with keys X, Y, Z (specify). Story is blocked until one outcome is confirmed and the plan updated.' This converts the placeholder into a bounded spike with a complete design for each branch.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the `feature-branch-drivers` membership criteria directly in the plan (e.g., 'human engineers with write access to the repo, added by Administrators via a tracked request; service accounts excluded; membership reviewed quarterly') so the ADR has concrete policy to record rather than leaving it to executor discretion.
findings.finding: The `feature-branch-drivers` group membership policy is described only as 'recorded in the ADR' with zero constraints specified in the plan. An executor writing the ADR has no guidance on who qualifies (e.g., any Gerrit registered user? only service accounts? only named humans?), what the approval process is, or what the maximum group size policy should be. The plan explicitly says 'the ACL bounds attack surface + review-cost amplification', implying the membership policy is security-relevant — yet the policy itself is left entirely unspecified.
findings.location: Locked design — ACL section; S1 AC — 'membership policy recorded in the ADR'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In the `assemble_merge_change_context` specification, explicitly state the format of per-file diff content passed under `## Auto-merge diff`: confirm that `get_file_diff` output is rendered to standard unified diff format (including `diff --git a/… b/…` headers) so that `changed_from_diff` correctly extracts file paths for `apply_failclosed` security-detector scanning, OR document that `changed_files` will be passed separately to `produce_code_review_verdict` alongside `diff_text` to bypass `changed_from_diff` parsing.
findings.finding: The plan's S2 merge-change review path uses per-file REST diffs (`get_file_diff`) stitched into a `## Merge context` / `## Auto-merge diff` markdown composite and passes this as `diff_text` through the existing `adapter.code_review_decision` seam. Inside `produce_code_review_verdict`, `assemble_diff_context(diff_text=…)` is called, which uses `changed_from_diff(diff_text)` (assemble.py lines 49–64) to parse file paths by looking for `diff --git a/… b/…` headers. The plan's assembled string wraps diff content in markdown headings (`## Auto-merge diff`) and commit subject lines — the plan never specifies whether the stitched per-file diffs preserve standard `diff --git` headers or whether Gerrit's REST diff format includes them. If headers are absent or the markdown wrapping disrupts parsing, `changed_files` will be empty, disabling `apply_failclosed` security-detector scanning on merge changes. The plan's positive claim that 'data flow into the gate is unchanged' masks this format-compatibility dependency.
findings.location: S2 — assemble_merge_change_context specification; assemble.py:changed_from_diff; gate_dispatch.py lines 436–476

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and document how `assemble_merge_change_context` output (containing `## Merge context` / `## Auto-merge diff` headers) interacts with `produce_code_review_verdict`'s internal `DiffContext` wrapping; either (a) bypass `assemble_diff_context` for merge changes and pass the assembled context directly to the gate runner, or (b) produce a plain unified-diff string from auto-merge files so `DiffContext.context` wraps it correctly, and add a unit test asserting the final kernel context string has the expected structure for a merge-change input.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'data flow into the gate is unchanged: the assembled string is passed as `diff_text` through the existing seam — `adapter.code_review_decision(diff_text=…)` → `produce_code_review_verdict(cfg, diff_text=…)` — no runner/gate-machinery changes.' However, `produce_code_review_verdict` in `gate_dispatch.py` internally passes `diff_text` to `assemble.assemble_diff_context(diff_text=diff_text)`, which wraps it inside `DiffContext.context` as `## Changed files / ## Orientation / ## Diff` markdown. Passing the new `assemble_merge_change_context` output (which already contains `## Merge context` and `## Auto-merge diff` headers) as `diff_text` will cause the gate to double-wrap this content — the merge-context structure becomes nested inside a `## Diff` block, with a misleading `## Changed files` header parsed from the merge-context markdown. The plan does not verify that the gate kernel's prompts (`gates/code-review.yaml`) handle this nested format correctly, and does not acknowledge the wrapping.
findings.location: S2 — Review-bot merge-change path, paragraph 'Data flow into the gate is unchanged'; `src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py` line 170–174; `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py` lines 392–430; `src/rebar/llm/code_review/assemble.py` lines 77–93

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that epic 1fa8 (smoky-flare-slug) is actually merged by confirming: (a) `infra/gerrit/project.config` contains a `[label "Verified"]` block and a two-vote `submittableIf`, and (b) `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` exists — if either is absent, gate this plan on 1fa8 completion before execution.
findings.finding: The plan's foundational prerequisite asserts 'the Verified label exists, cast by GitHub Actions via gerrit-to-platform hooks … the gerrit-verify.yaml workflow exists, and the two-vote submit requirement label:LLM-Review=MAX AND label:Verified=MAX AND -has:unresolved is active.' Reading the actual infra/gerrit/project.config shows only the pre-1fa8 single-vote setup: submittableIf = label:LLM-Review=MAX AND -has:unresolved, with no Verified label section at all. No gerrit-verify.yaml is found in .github/. The 1fa8 deliverables this plan explicitly depends on are not present in the committed codebase.
findings.location: Context section — 'ASSUMES epic 1fa8 (smoky-flare-slug) is completed and merged'; infra/gerrit/project.config; S1 AC (references Verified copyCondition UNCHANGED); S3 AC (assumes 1fa8 live)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a concrete 'gate check' step to S1 (or as a pre-S1 step): query the running Gerrit via REST for the Verified label config, and confirm gerrit-verify.yaml exists in the repo, before any S1 config push. If absent, block the epic and track 1fa8 to done first.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to S2 a named greppable journald marker string (e.g. `MERGE_CHANGE_ERROR`) that voter.py emits for all merge-path errors, parallel to `VOTER_ERROR`, and specify that `observability.sh` greps for this exact string to compute the `review_bot_merge_change_errors` count.
findings.finding: S2 specifies publishing a new CloudWatch metric `review_bot_merge_change_errors` via `infra/scripts/observability.sh` 'exactly as `voter_errors` is'. In `observability.sh`, `voter_errors` is published by grepping journald for the literal string `VOTER_ERROR`. The plan names structured log events `merge_change_review`, `merge_change_409_guard`, and `mergelist_fetch_error` (emitted by voter.py) but never specifies a single greppable journald marker string analogous to `VOTER_ERROR` for the observability.sh grep to match against. Without this, the `observability.sh` edit cannot be implemented as specified.
findings.location: S2 — Observability / infra/scripts/observability.sh
findings.suggested_fix: Adopt `MERGE_CHANGE_ERROR` as the marker (emitted by a `_merge_change_error()` helper parallel to `_voter_error()`), and add a section to `observability.sh` grepping `CONTAINER_NAME="$VOTER_CONTAINER"` journald output for `MERGE_CHANGE_ERROR`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the `review_bot_merge_change_errors` observability spec in S2: either drop 'reason-tagged' and publish a single dimensionless count per named error marker (matching the `voter_errors` pattern — three separate `metric-name` values, one per grep pattern), or specify the exact CloudWatch dimension scheme and update the monitoring TF to declare those dimensions.
findings.finding: The plan specifies that `review_bot_merge_change_errors` should be a 'reason-tagged' CloudWatch metric published 'via `infra/scripts/observability.sh` exactly as `voter_errors` is.' The existing `voter_errors` metric in `observability.sh` (lines 103-119) is a dimensionless count published via a grep of `VOTER_ERROR` markers in journald — it carries no reason dimension. CloudWatch does not support dynamic dimension values on a single `put-metric-data` call in a simple count pattern; reason-tagging requires either separate `MetricName` values per reason or CloudWatch metric dimensions (which must be declared statically in monitoring TF). The 'exactly as voter_errors is' description is therefore technically inconsistent with 'reason-tagged.'
findings.location: S2 — 'published as a CloudWatch metric `review_bot_merge_change_errors` (reason-tagged) via `infra/scripts/observability.sh` exactly as `voter_errors` is'; infra/scripts/observability.sh:92-119
findings.suggested_fix: Use three separate journald-grep metrics mirroring the `voter_errors` pattern: `merge_change_409_errors`, `merge_change_files_errors`, `mergelist_fetch_errors` — each a simple dimensionless count. Remove 'reason-tagged' from the spec.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale in S2 for why the REST `files/` fan-out approach is preferred over running `git diff HEAD^1..HEAD` (first-parent) on the already-cloned merge commit working tree — if the REST approach is retained, document the specific advantages (e.g., avoids git history depth issues, provides file-level metadata, handles octopus merges) that outweigh the files/-default-base assumption risk; if on reflection the git-diff approach is simpler and equally correct, consider adopting it.
findings.finding: The plan's chosen mechanism for merge-change diff fetching is a REST `files/` fan-out (one `get_file_diff` call per changed file). An alternative that avoids the REST-default-base assumption (the plan's own 'riskiest assumption') is: after `clone_change_ref` fetches the merge commit, run `git diff HEAD^1..HEAD` (or `git show --format= -p --diff-merges=first-parent HEAD`) on the cloned working tree to get the first-parent diff as a standard unified diff. This eliminates the `files/` default-base REST assumption entirely, produces a diff format the existing `changed_from_diff` / `apply_failclosed` / `compose_diff_context` pipeline consumes unchanged (no format-compatibility risk), and requires zero new `gerrit_client.py` methods. The plan states the files/-default is the 'riskiest assumption' but never explains why REST fan-out is preferred over this git-diff alternative.
findings.location: S2 — Review-bot merge-change path; Sequencing & risk section ('riskiest assumption'); Decision log (REST mechanics)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in S2 whether the new gerrit_client methods (get_commit, get_merge_files, get_file_diff, get_mergelist) inherit existing retry/backoff or require explicit wiring, and document the decision; if the existing client has no retry, note that the multi-step fan-out increases transient-error exposure and consider at minimum a single-retry-with-backoff on 5xx/timeout for each new method.
findings.finding: The new gerrit_client.py methods (get_commit, get_merge_files, get_file_diff, get_mergelist) are called in a sequential fan-out per merge-change review, but the plan does not specify whether these methods inherit the existing retry/backoff logic from gerrit_client.py or require explicit wiring. On a multi-step fan-out path, a single transient Gerrit API error (5xx, timeout) in any of the four calls produces a permanent fail-closed -1, requiring a manual /rerun. This is a higher operational burden than the existing single-call path, with no mitigation stated.
findings.location: S2 — gerrit_client.py new methods; S2 error handling spec ('ANY merge-path REST failure → fail-closed -1')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the S2 spec: 'The new gerrit_client methods reuse the existing `_request_with_retry` (or equivalent) wrapper; if no such wrapper exists, each new method applies a single exponential-backoff retry on HTTP 5xx and timeout before failing closed.' This keeps the posture consistent and reduces /rerun noise without changing the fail-closed guarantee.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm` resource (e.g. in a new `monitoring_merge.tf`) for the `review_bot_merge_change_errors` metric, wired to `aws_sns_topic.alerts`, mirroring the shape of `monitoring_s4b.tf`'s `voter_errors` alarm.
findings.finding: The plan specifies a new CloudWatch metric `review_bot_merge_change_errors` published via `infra/scripts/observability.sh` but does not call out a corresponding `aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm` IaC resource for it. Every other operationally important metric in the repo (voter_errors → monitoring_s4b.tf, replication_errors → monitoring_s5.tf, mirror_out_of_sync → monitoring_ws7.tf, GerritReachable → monitoring.tf) has a paired alarm resource; without an alarm, the merge-change error metric is dark and operators must poll CloudWatch manually to notice a broken merge-change path.
findings.location: S2 AC — 'published as a CloudWatch metric review_bot_merge_change_errors (reason-tagged) via infra/scripts/observability.sh exactly as voter_errors is'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/test_review_bot.py` to S2's explicit edit-target list and specify that `_FakeGerrit` must be extended with stub implementations of `get_commit`, `get_merge_files`, `get_file_diff`, and `get_mergelist` (and a 409-raising variant for the fail-closed guard test).
findings.finding: The test mock class `_FakeGerrit` in `tests/unit/test_review_bot.py` (lines 50–71) implements only `has_llm_review_vote`, `clone_change_ref`, `get_patch`, and `post_vote`. S2 adds four new `GerritClient` methods (`get_commit`, `get_merge_files`, `get_file_diff`, `get_mergelist`) that the voter's new merge path will call, but `tests/unit/test_review_bot.py` is not named as an edit target anywhere in the plan. Tests for the new merge path (including the '409 path stays fail-closed' assertion and the 'reconciler backfill routes through merge detection' test) cannot be written without updating this mock.
findings.location: S2 — Review-bot merge-change path / tests/unit/test_review_bot.py

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a named constant `MERGELIST_CHAR_CAP` (e.g. a fixed fraction of `DIFF_CHAR_CAP` such as `DIFF_CHAR_CAP // 4`) and specify in the `assemble_merge_change_context` contract that the mergelist section is capped at `min(MERGELIST_MAX_COMMITS commits, MERGELIST_CHAR_CAP chars)` before the remainder of `DIFF_CHAR_CAP` is allocated to the auto-merge diff.
findings.finding: The combined-cap protocol in `assemble_merge_change_context` has ambiguous placement: the plan defines `MERGELIST_MAX_COMMITS = 100` (a count cap) AND states 'the COMBINED string is capped at DIFF_CHAR_CAP with the mergelist section capped first', but never specifies a character budget for the mergelist section. When 100 commit subject lines fill most of `DIFF_CHAR_CAP = 60000` chars, the decision rule for how much space remains for the auto-merge diff is undefined — there is no named constant or formula for the mergelist character cap, only the count constant.
findings.location: S2 — 'Merge-context contract' paragraph (combined cap description); src/rebar/llm/code_review/assemble.py:29 (DIFF_CHAR_CAP = 60000)
findings.suggested_fix: Add `MERGELIST_CHAR_CAP = DIFF_CHAR_CAP // 4  # 15 000 chars` to `assemble.py` alongside `DIFF_CHAR_CAP`, and reference it explicitly in the S2 contract description.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `review_bot_merge_change_errors` provides distinct alerting value over the existing `voter_errors` metric; if merge-change REST failures will route through `_voter_error()` (as described in S2), drop the separate metric and instead add a `reason=merge_change` field to the existing `VOTER_ERROR` structured log line so operators can filter in CloudWatch Logs Insights without a new metric.
findings.finding: The plan adds a new CloudWatch metric `review_bot_merge_change_errors` (reason-tagged, published via `observability.sh`) covering merge-change REST failures. However, the existing architecture already handles this: merge-change REST failures in `voter.py` would go through the existing `_voter_error()` path (same fail-closed posture described in S2: 'ANY merge-path REST failure → fail-closed -1 carrying the existing coverage-gap (infra) message tag'), which already writes `VOTER_ERROR` to stderr and is already counted by `observability.sh §4 → rebar/host:voter_errors → rebar-gerrit-voter-errors alarm`. The new metric is a subset of `voter_errors`, adding a second CloudWatch metric + alarm for events already surfaced by the first — config-surface proliferation without demonstrably distinct alerting value.
findings.location: S2 — Observability: `review_bot_merge_change_errors` metric; `infra/scripts/observability.sh`
findings.suggested_fix: In the merge-change error path, call `_voter_error(..., reason='merge_change_rest_failure')` — the existing `VOTER_ERROR` journald grep picks it up for `voter_errors`, and the reason field is filterable in CloudWatch Logs Insights without a new metric or alarm resource.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit activation gate (e.g., `MERGE_CHANGE_REVIEW_ENABLED` env-var defaulting to `false`, flipped to `true` only after S5 E2E passes) so the new voter.py merge path is deployed but disabled until validated, avoiding a 100%-traffic flip on day one.
findings.finding: The new merge-change code path in voter.py activates for 100% of merge-commit events immediately on bot redeploy, with no feature flag or canary — a single traffic flip to new behavior on a deployed long-running service.
findings.location: S2 — Review-bot merge-change path / Sequencing & risk section
findings.suggested_fix: In voter.py merge detection block, wrap the merge path in `if os.getenv('MERGE_CHANGE_REVIEW_ENABLED', 'false').lower() == 'true': ... else: log and return fail-closed -1 with tag 'merge_change_not_enabled'`. Deploy with flag off, run S5 E2E with flag on in the test environment, then flip flag in production. Document the flag and its flip as a step in S6.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state in the S1 ADR (and verify in S5 scenario 7) that `feature-branch-drivers` group membership is admin-managed-only (not self-service for Registered Users), and add a negative test confirming a non-member Registered User cannot push a merge commit to refs/for/refs/heads/main even with SSH access to Gerrit.
findings.finding: The plan grants `Push Merge Commit` on `refs/for/refs/heads/main` to the new `feature-branch-drivers` group — a privileged write path on a public-internet-reachable Gerrit (port 29418 open to 0.0.0.0/0) — but does not specify that the group is admin-managed-only (i.e., that no Registered User can self-add). The ADR is only said to 'record the membership policy,' with no stated enforcement mechanism, approval workflow, or audit trail for group membership changes. If group membership were misconfigured or escalated, an untrusted actor could push merge commits to refs/for/main, bypassing the 'narrow group' access intent.
findings.location: Locked design — 'feature-branch-drivers' group; S1 AC; S5 scenario 7 negative tests
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the S1 ADR section: 'Group membership in `feature-branch-drivers` requires explicit Gerrit Administrator action; Registered Users cannot self-add. Group membership changes are audited via refs/meta/config history.' Add to S5 scenario 7: a test where a non-member authenticated user attempts to push a merge commit to refs/for/main and is refused at the ACL level (not just the pushMerge-absent case, which may overlap — verify the group boundary holds independently).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit step to the S6 back-out section for the LLM-Review copyCondition change: either (a) include the revert command (`restore copyCondition to its pre-S1 value in project.config`) and verify via the running Gerrit, or (b) explicitly record that the condition is intentionally left in place on rollback with the rationale ('MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE is a no-op in the absence of merge commits') — and correct the 'NO label/gate changes' claim accordingly.
findings.finding: S6's back-out plan explicitly states 'revoke pushMerge + feature/* ACLs restores the single-change-only flow with NO label/gate changes', but S1 *does* change a label/gate config: it modifies LLM-Review's copyCondition from its current value to add `OR changekind:MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE` in project.config. The back-out plan never addresses reverting this label config change, leaving either an undocumented omission or an undocumented intentional choice (the condition is harmless when no merge commits exist, but this reasoning is not recorded).
findings.location: S6 — Process + back-out / S1 — Gate + ACL config (LLM-Review copyCondition)
findings.suggested_fix: In S6, replace 'NO label/gate changes' with 'NO label-vote or Verified config changes; the LLM-Review copyCondition addition (MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE) is intentionally left in place on ACL rollback because it is a no-op in the absence of merge commits — record this decision in the ADR.' If true rollback is desired, add: 'revert the copyCondition line in project.config to its S1-recorded prior value and push to All-Projects.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct S6's back-out description to include reverting `LLM-Review copyCondition` to `changekind:NO_CODE_CHANGE` (the S1-recorded prior value) as an explicit step alongside the ACL revocation and submit-type pin removal — drop the incorrect 'NO label/gate changes' claim.
findings.finding: S6 back-out states 'revoke pushMerge + feature/* ACLs restores the single-change-only flow with NO label/gate changes.' But S1 changes the `LLM-Review` label `copyCondition` in `project.config` from `changekind:NO_CODE_CHANGE` to `changekind:NO_CODE_CHANGE OR changekind:MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE`. That IS a label configuration change. S6 documents a separate 'submit-type pin back-out' but never mentions reverting the `copyCondition`. The claim of 'NO label/gate changes' on an ACL-only rollback is factually incorrect and will mislead an operator performing a partial rollback — they may believe they've fully restored the prior state when the label still carries the extended `copyCondition`.
findings.location: S6 — Process + back-out, back-out paragraph; project.config line 42 (copyCondition); S1 AC
findings.suggested_fix: In S6, replace 'revoke pushMerge + feature/* ACLs restores the single-change-only flow with NO label/gate changes' with 'revoke pushMerge + feature/* ACLs AND revert LLM-Review copyCondition to changekind:NO_CODE_CHANGE (the S1-recorded prior value) restores the single-change-only flow.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm in S2's implementation spec that the voter.py edit replaces the unconditional `gc.get_patch` call at line 216 with a branching flow (merge-path vs. normal-path), not merely inserts a detection check before an otherwise-unchanged get_patch call.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that voter.py's merge detection runs 'INSIDE review_and_vote immediately after the existing-vote check and BEFORE any diff fetch.' However, reading voter.py confirms the current code structure is: existing-vote check (lines 175-208) → then a single block (lines 210-233) that unconditionally calls gc.get_patch (line 216). There is no conditional branch for merge detection. The plan's S2 description says the merge path is inserted at a specific point, but the actual code has a flat linear flow where gc.get_patch is the only diff-fetch call — meaning the S2 edit target is voter.py lines ~210-233, NOT a new branch inserted 'after the existing-vote check', but rather a replacement of the entire diff-fetch block.
findings.location: S2 AC — 'merge detection via revisions/{rev}/commit parent count (>=2) runs INSIDE review_and_vote immediately after the existing-vote check and BEFORE any diff fetch'; voter.py lines 210-233
findings.suggested_fix: Update the S2 edit description to: 'voter.py — replace the unconditional gc.get_patch call (currently line 216) with a merge-detection branch: if parent count ≥ 2, take the merge path (get_merge_files/get_mergelist); otherwise call gc.get_patch as before.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `assemble_merge_change_context` needs to live in `assemble.py` (one call-site violates Rule-of-Three for a module-level extraction); if no second call-site is anticipated before this epic closes, move it to a private helper in `voter.py` or a new `src/rebar/review_bot/_merge.py` sibling, and import `DIFF_CHAR_CAP` from `assemble` where needed.
findings.finding: `assemble_merge_change_context` proposed in `assemble.py` has a single call-site (the merge detection branch in `voter.py`), which is premature abstraction by the Rule-of-Three. The function's co-location in `assemble.py` is justified only by proximity to `DIFF_CHAR_CAP` and `DiffContext`, both of which are importable from that module — not a cohesion argument strong enough to warrant a module-boundary extraction with one call-site. The logic could live as a private helper inside `voter.py` (or in a `_merge.py` sibling to `voter.py`) until a second call-site materialises.
findings.location: S2 — Merge-context contract: `assemble_merge_change_context` in `src/rebar/llm/code_review/assemble.py`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the adapter.py `_TAG_SUFFIXES` ambiguity in S2: either explicitly allow a new key (e.g. `'merge-PASS'`) or specify that `_message_tag` gains a `context_suffix: str = ''` parameter — and name any test that pins the `_TAG_SUFFIXES` vocabulary as an edit target.
findings.finding: The plan states that the merge-change review comment 'REUSES the existing `_TAG_SUFFIXES` verdict keys in `adapter.py`, appending `(merge-change, N integrated commits)` to the existing suffix text (no new verdict vocabulary)'. However, `_message_tag()` in `adapter.py` performs a direct dict key lookup (`_TAG_SUFFIXES[reason]`), so appending context-dependent text to the returned suffix value without a new key requires either a new key (new vocabulary) or changing `_message_tag`'s signature to accept runtime context. The plan's constraint 'no new verdict vocabulary' conflicts with its requirement for a distinct merge-change suffix — the implementation path is genuinely ambiguous.
findings.location: S2 — Robot comment spec / src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py lines 46–66

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how `_message_tag` / `_TAG_SUFFIXES` is extended to carry a runtime `N` integrated-commit count in the merge-change robot comment suffix — the static dict cannot interpolate `N`; define the concrete mechanism (e.g., a parameterized `_merge_message_tag(reason, n_commits)` helper that formats the tag without touching `_TAG_SUFFIXES`) and confirm it leaves the existing non-merge tag format and its assertion test unchanged.
findings.finding: The plan says the merge-change verdict 'REUSES the existing `_TAG_SUFFIXES` verdict keys in `adapter.py`, appending `(merge-change, N integrated commits)` to the existing suffix text (no new verdict vocabulary).' But `_TAG_SUFFIXES` in `adapter.py` is a static `dict[str, str]` (lines 46–53) where the PASS suffix is the literal string `'PASS'`. Producing `'PASS (merge-change, 12 integrated commits)'` requires runtime interpolation of `N` — which cannot be done by appending to a static dict value without either (a) adding a new key or (b) changing `_message_tag` to accept runtime kwargs. The plan's 'no new verdict vocabulary' constraint directly contradicts the need for a parameterized runtime suffix. As written, the mechanism will not produce the intended robot comment.
findings.location: S2 — Review-bot merge-change path, robot comment paragraph; adapter.py lines 46–53 (_TAG_SUFFIXES) and _message_tag()
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `_merge_message_tag(reason: str, n_commits: int, *, label: str = 'LLM-Review') -> str` helper in adapter.py that formats `f'[{label}: {_TAG_SUFFIXES[reason]} (merge-change, {n_commits} integrated commits)]'`, called only on the merge path, leaving `_message_tag` and `_TAG_SUFFIXES` untouched. The plan's claim 'no new verdict vocabulary' is satisfied because the _TAG_SUFFIXES key set is unchanged.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `assemble_merge_change_context`'s output, when passed as `diff_text` to `produce_code_review_verdict`, produces a correct `## Changed files` section in the `CodeReviewBatchRunner` context — specifically that `changed_from_diff()` applied to the `## Merge context … ## Auto-merge diff <unified-diff>` string extracts the correct file list; if not, either supply `changed_files=` explicitly from `get_merge_files()` results, or add a `changed_files` parameter to `assemble_merge_change_context` that is forwarded to `produce_code_review_verdict`.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'data flow into the gate is unchanged: the assembled string is passed as `diff_text` through the existing seam' for merge changes, but `produce_code_review_verdict` (gate_dispatch.py:436-438) calls `assemble.assemble_diff_context(diff_text=...)` which in turn calls `changed_from_diff()` to parse a standard unified diff header (`diff --git a/… b/…` or `+++ b/…`). The new `assemble_merge_change_context` output is formatted as `## Merge context … ## Auto-merge diff` markdown — not a bare unified diff — so `changed_from_diff()` will return an empty or wrong file list, meaning the `CodeReviewBatchRunner` context loses the `## Changed files` section for merge changes, silently degrading the review quality.
findings.location: S2 — 'Data flow into the gate is unchanged'; src/rebar/llm/code_review/assemble.py:49-64 (`changed_from_diff`); src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py:436-438
findings.suggested_fix: Pass the file list from `get_merge_files()` explicitly as `changed_files=` to `produce_code_review_verdict` (it accepts `changed_files: list[str] | None`) rather than relying on `changed_from_diff()` to parse the merged context string.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] S2 must specify the exact mechanism for appending `(merge-change, N integrated commits)` to the robot-comment tag — either add a new `_TAG_SUFFIXES` key (and state the key string), or add a `note: str = ''` parameter to `_message_tag`, or define a wrapper function `_merge_message_tag(reason, n)` — so the closed dict's 'asserted by a test' contract remains intact and no implementer has to guess.
findings.finding: The plan says the merge-change robot comment REUSES existing `_TAG_SUFFIXES` keys (e.g. 'PASS') but appends `(merge-change, N integrated commits)` to the suffix text. `_message_tag(reason)` directly indexes `_TAG_SUFFIXES[reason]` (no `.get()`, no parameter for annotation), so the appended text cannot be produced without either (a) a new key in the closed dict, (b) a new wrapper, or (c) a new parameter. The plan never specifies which approach is taken, making this a referenced-but-undefined schema extension that will cause a KeyError or produce malformed output if a new key is accidentally used.
findings.location: S2 — 'Robot comment' paragraph; src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py:46–53 (_TAG_SUFFIXES)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `note: str = ''` parameter to `_message_tag`: `return f"[{label}: {_TAG_SUFFIXES[reason]}{' ' + note if note else ''}]"`. Then in the merge path call `_message_tag(reason, note=f'merge-change, {n} integrated commits')`. No new key is needed; the existing test that pins `_TAG_SUFFIXES` keys remains valid.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
